zh.lifeSummary: Barbara Bridge於1593年出生在英國英格蘭艾可納。她於大約1624年與John Ruggles Sr在英國艾可納結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1636年11月1日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可享年43歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州素福爾可波斯頓洛波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Barbara Bridge于1593年出生在英国英格兰艾可納。她于大约1624年与John Ruggles Sr在英国艾可納结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1636年11月1日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可享年43岁，埋葬在美国马萨诸塞州素福爾可波斯頓洛波利。

nameMeanings.name: Bridge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English brigge ‘bridge’, Old English brycg, applied as a topographic name for someone who lived near a bridge, a metonymic occupational name for a bridge keeper, or a habitational name from any of the places called with this element, as for example Bridge in Kent or Bridge Sollers in Herefordshire. Building and maintaining bridges was one of the three main feudal obligations, along with bearing arms and maintaining fortifications. The cost of building a bridge was often defrayed by charging a toll, the surname thus being acquired by the toll gatherer.

variants.name: Bridges
meanings: English: variant of Bridge . The -s generally represents the genitive case, but may occasionally be a plural. In some cases this name denoted someone from the Flemish city of Bruges (Brugge) in Belgium, meaning ‘bridges’, which had extensive trading links with England in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Bridger
meanings: English: from an agent derivative of Middle English brigge, brugge ‘bridge’ (Old English brycg), denoting either someone who lived near a bridge or the keeper of a bridge. See Bridge .

variants.name: Burge
meanings: English: variant of Bridge , Old English brycg, with metathesis of u and r, as exemplified in several placenames of this origin in various parts of southern England.
Swiss German and German (Bürge): from Middle High German bürge ‘bailsman, guarantor’. Compare Buerge .

variants.name: Bridgeman
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English brigge, brugge ‘bridge’ (Old English brycg) + man, denoting either a dweller by or a keeper of a bridge. See Bridge .
In some cases possibly also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German Bruckmann (see Bruckman ).
History: James Bridgeman or Bridgman (1620–76) came to Hartford, CT, from Winchester, Hampshire, England, in 1640.

variants.name: Brugman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Erna, Kurt.
Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived near a bridge or a metonymic occupational name for a bridge keeper, from Dutch brug ‘bridge’ (see Bridge ). Compare Bruggeman .
Americanized form of Swiss German Bruggmann ‘bridge keeper’.

nameMeanings.name: Barbara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin, meaning ‘foreign woman’ (a feminine form of barbarus ‘foreign’, from Greek, referring originally to the unintelligible chatter of foreigners, which sounded to the Greek ear like no more than bar-bar). St Barbara has always been one of the most popular saints in the calendar, although there is some doubt whether she ever actually existed. According to legend, she was imprisoned in a tower and later murdered by her father, who was then struck down by a bolt of lightning. Accordingly, she is the patron of architects, stonemasons, and fortifications, and of firework makers, artillerymen, and gunpowder magazines.

variants.name: Barbra
meanings: From Latin, meaning ‘foreign woman’ (a feminine form of barbarus ‘foreign’, from Greek, referring originally to the unintelligible chatter of foreigners, which sounded to the Greek ear like no more than bar-bar). St Barbara has always been one of the most popular saints in the calendar, although there is some doubt whether she ever actually existed. According to legend, she was imprisoned in a tower and later murdered by her father, who was then struck down by a bolt of lightning. Accordingly, she is the patron of architects, stonemasons, and fortifications, and of firework makers, artillerymen, and gunpowder magazines.

en.lifeSummary: Barbara Bridge was born in 1593, in Nazeing, Essex, England, United Kingdom. She married John Ruggles Sr about 1624, in Nazeing, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 1 November 1636, in Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 43, and was buried in Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Barbara Bridge est née en 1593, à Nazeing, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé John Ruggles Sr environ 1624, à Nazeing, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 1 novembre 1636, à Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterrée à Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, États-Unis.

ja.lifeSummary: Barbara Bridgeは1593年にイギリスイギリスエセックスナズエイングで生まれた。約1624年にイギリスエセックスナズエイングでJohn Ruggles Srと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1636年11月1日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクで43歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州スフォルクボストンロクスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Barbara Bridge(은)는 1593년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 나즈인그에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1624년 영국 에세스 나즈인그에서 John Ruggles Sr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1636년 11월 1일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 수폴크 보스턴 록스버이에 묻혔다.

Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Barbara Bridge родилась в 1593 г. в местности назинг, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Ruggles Sr около 1624 г. в местности назинг, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 1 ноября 1636 г. в местности суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 43 лет и была похоронена в местности роксбури, бостон, суффолк, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.
